title: rosa × collina var. caesia
intro: Rosa × collina var. caesia, also known as the purple rose or Caesia rose, is a variety of rose plant that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant is a type of wild rose, characterized by its fragrant, dark green leaves and small, fragrant flowers that are typically a deep purple color. The medicinal properties of Rosa × collina var. caesia include its high content of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Rosa × collina var. caesia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and other infections, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
